channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Scrapping higher insurance rebates for over-65s raises pressure on public hospital funding
item.description: The federal government’s decision to remove higher private health insurance rebates for Australians aged over 65 is expected to place significant financial pressure on public hospitals, potentially co...

item.title: Montreal Health Officials Report Surge in Overdoses Linked to Fentanyl Mixtures
item.description: Montreal public health officials are issuing a serious warning regarding a sharp increase in drug overdoses within the city. Since late March, authorities have observed a significant rise in non-fatal...

item.title: KimPau captivates fans during their Redwood City stop in world tour
item.description: Kim Chiu and Paulo Avelino, collectively known as KimPau, continue to enchant their fans during their world tour, with their recent stop in Redwood City. The Filipino celebrity couple greeted thousand...

item.title: Teenager extradited from UAE charged with Westminster murder
item.description: An 18-year-old man, Enzo Bettamio, has appeared in Westminster Magistrates' Court after being extradited from the United Arab Emirates to face charges for the murder of Kamonnan Thiamphanit, also know...

item.title: Seven KwaZulu-Natal family members to be laid to rest after abduction and brutal killings
item.description: Seven members of the Monswamy family will be laid to rest in KwaDukuza, KwaZulu-Natal, following a horrific abduction and murder case that has shaken the community. The victims include Alan Monswamy, ...

item.title: Ogoni Cleanup Progress and Challenges: Oil Exploration Remains on Hold
item.description: Mr. Celestine Akpobari, an environmental rights activist, discusses the ongoing cleanup of Ogoni land and the barriers to resuming oil exploration in the region. Despite the efforts made by the Hydroc...

item.title: Manitoba Métis President Responds to AFN Chief’s Suggestion on Treaty Withdrawal
item.description: The President of the Manitoba Métis Federation has publicly criticized the Chief of the Assembly of First Nations (AFN) following remarks suggesting the withdrawal from a historic treaty. The Métis le...

item.title: Nigeria and South Africa deepen partnership to strengthen energy infrastructure and investment
item.description: Nigeria and South Africa don agree say stronger collaboration for energy sector dey very important if Africa wan grow into a global energy powerhouse. Dis matter come up during the fourth South Africa...

item.title: Global report warns conflict, drought and reduced aid will worsen food insecurity in 2026
item.description: A new global report on food crises has warned that hunger will remain at critical levels in 2026, driven by ongoing conflict, worsening drought conditions and declining humanitarian aid. The report hi...

item.title: Man critically injured in West Toronto shooting
item.description: Authorities in Toronto are investigating a shooting incident in the city's west end that left one man in critical condition. Police have not yet released the identity of the victim or detailed informa...

item.title: NBI Investigates Alleged Threats Against President Marcos, First Lady, and Former Speaker Romualdez

item.title: RAF jets respond to Russian drone threat near Nato border in Romania
item.description: Two RAF Eurofighter Typhoon jets were scrambled early on a Saturday morning to address a potential threat from Russian drones near the Nato border in Romania. The Romanian Defence Ministry reported th...

item.title: Ollie Hollands Withdrawn from Fremantle Game Due to Family Struggles
item.description: Carlton Football Club has decided to withdraw Ollie Hollands from their match against Fremantle at Optus Stadium following a difficult week for the young player and his family. Ollie, who is the broth...

item.title: The impact of financial imbalances on relationships
item.description: In relationships, financial instability can sometimes become the breaking point, and it’s often overlooked. A key issue in many relationships is when one partner’s spending habits, or hidden debts, th...

item.title: Kenyan Man Dies in Motorcycle Accident in the US, 3 Years after Parents Were Murdered
item.description: Joseph Nyagechi Morema, a 29-year-old Kenyan man living in the US, tragically lost his life in a motorcycle accident in Eagan, Minnesota, on April 21, 2026. This comes just three years after his paren...

item.title: Ghana Mineworkers Raise Concerns Over Local Contractor Policy Impacting Pay and Job Security
item.description: Ghana’s union of mineworkers has expressed strong opposition to a government policy mandating international mining companies to hire local firms as contractors. Union president Abdul Moomin Gbana high...

item.title: Heatwave grips North and Central India as temperatures cross 40°C; IMD issues alerts for multiple regions
item.description: Large parts of North, Central and Eastern India are currently experiencing severe heatwave conditions, with temperatures crossing 40°C in several regions. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has...

item.title: Pedestrian Hospitalized in Critical Condition After Brampton Nighttime Collision
item.description: A pedestrian in Brampton suffered critical injuries following a late-night traffic collision. Authorities are investigating the circumstances surrounding the incident, including potential factors such...

item.title: ‘Gen V’ ends after two seasons as storyline shifts into The Boys Season 5
item.description: The superhero series Gen V, wey be spinoff from the popular show The Boys, don officially end after just two seasons. Even though some fans no expect am, the decision actually come from a mix of real-...

item.title: Gemma Collins and Sinitta exit stage during tense 'I'm A Celeb' final, receiving fan praise
item.description: During the live final of 'I'm A Celebrity... All Stars', former rivals Gemma Collins and Sinitta made a notable exit from the stage, leaving their campmates to continue a heated dispute. The incident ...
